findings.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer package-private test constructor accepts executor/nounCqlPattern without null validation, unlike the production injection constructor
evidence: Test constructor (line 70): `SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor executor, String nounCqlPattern)` has no Objects.requireNonNull calls and no @NonNull annotations on parameters

Production injection constructor (lines 90-100): `Objects.requireNonNull(executor, ...)`, `Objects.requireNonNull(comparator, ...)`, `Objects.requireNonNull(multiSeedExplorer, ...)`, `Objects.requireNonNull(grammarConfig, ...)` are all present

A null executor passed to the test constructor is silently stored and propagated into CollocateProfileComparator, MultiSeedExplorer, and SingleSeedExplorer, where it will cause an NPE at query time with no descriptive message
findings.suggestion: Add explicit null guards in the test constructor: `Objects.requireNonNull(executor, "executor must not be null")` and `Objects.requireNonNull(nounCqlPattern, "nounCqlPattern must not be null")`. Add @NonNull annotations to both parameters to make the contract visible.

findings.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer stores a QueryExecutor field used only in fetchExamples, while exploreByRelation/exploreMultiSeed/compareCollocateProfiles all fully delegate to dedicated sub-components
evidence: `fetchExamples` (lines 167-193) is directly implemented on SemanticFieldExplorer itself via `executor.executeBcqlQuery(bcqlQuery, maxExamples)` — breaking the uniform delegation/facade pattern
Private field `private final QueryExecutor executor` (line 54) exists solely to service `fetchExamples`; all other delegation paths never touch it
findings.suggestion: Extract the examples-fetch logic into a dedicated `ExamplesFetcher` package-private class (parallel to SingleSeedExplorer, MultiSeedExplorer, CollocateProfileComparator) and delegate from SemanticFieldExplorer. This makes the facade pattern consistent across all four service methods and removes the dual role of the class.

findings.summary: ExplorationService interface Javadoc @throws ExplorationException for all four methods, but no interface method signature declares it; SemanticFieldExplorer implementation does declare it — asymmetric contract visibility
evidence: ExplorationService.exploreByRelation (line 36-40): no `throws` clause in signature, but Javadoc says `@throws pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.exploration.ExplorationException if corpus access fails`
Same pattern repeated for exploreMultiSeed (line 47), compareCollocateProfiles (line 58), fetchExamples (line 68) — all document throws in Javadoc but omit from signature

ExplorationHandlers catches ExplorationException via `catch (ExplorationException e)` on a reference of type ExplorationService — works at runtime since ExplorationException is unchecked, but IDE shows no throws declaration on the interface method, defeating the documented contract
findings.suggestion: Add `throws ExplorationException` to all four ExplorationService interface method signatures. Since ExplorationException extends RuntimeException it is syntactically permitted; adding it to the interface signature makes the documented exception contract visible to callers who hold an ExplorationService reference, consistent with what the implementation already declares.

findings.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.executeCqlQuery explicitly catches NullPointerException to re-throw it unchanged — catch-rethrow adds noise when the goal is only to prevent the generic RuntimeException handler below from wrapping it
evidence: Lines 152-154: `catch (NullPointerException e) { // Re-throw NPEs immediately — they are programming bugs, not expected runtime errors. throw e; }`
This is immediately followed by `catch (RuntimeException e) { throw new IOException("Unexpected error...", e); }` — the NPE handler exists purely to skip the wrapping in that handler
The pattern is unusual: a reader must understand the multi-catch ordering to see why NPE is singled out; the comment helps but the pattern itself is non-idiomatic
findings.suggestion: Replace the two-catch pattern with a single RuntimeException handler that checks for NullPointerException before wrapping: `catch (RuntimeException e) { if (e instanceof NullPointerException npe) throw npe; throw new IOException("Unexpected error executing CQL query: " + e.getMessage(), e); }`. Alternatively, move the NPE rethrow into an `instanceof`-guarded branch inside the single catch. Both alternatives read as intentional without relying on catch-clause ordering.

findings.summary: DiscoveredNoun and CoreCollocate record components carry no @NonNull/@Nullable annotations, inconsistent with WordSketchResult which annotates both key fields — nullability is undocumented for core domain model types
evidence: WordSketchResult (lines 7-8): `@NonNull String lemma`, `@NonNull String pos` — nullability explicitly documented
DiscoveredNoun: `String noun`, `Map<String, Double> sharedCollocates` — no annotations; a null noun would silently propagate into API responses (DiscoveredNounEntry.noun)
CoreCollocate: `String collocate` — no annotation; a null collocate flows into CoreCollocateEntry and then into JSON output

CollocateProfileComparator constructor `(QueryExecutor executor, GrammarConfig grammarConfig)` has no @NonNull/@Nullable on either parameter, unlike SemanticFieldExplorer's production constructors which annotate all parameters
findings.suggestion: Add `@NonNull` to the primary string identifier components of DiscoveredNoun (`noun`), CoreCollocate (`collocate`), and the Map components where null is not a valid value. Apply the same treatment to the `executor` parameter of CollocateProfileComparator's constructor. This aligns with the project's established jspecify annotation convention visible in WordSketchResult, QueryExecutor, and ExplorationService.
